title: cerastium pentandrum subsp. obscurum
intro: Cerastium pentandrum subsp. obscurum, also known as the Obscure Mouse-ear Chickweed, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Cerastium pentandrum subsp. obscurum include its ability to soothe and heal skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
